trade.summary: The US is Germany's second-largest trading partner. Germany has a large trade surplus with the US, primarily from automotive exports (BMW, Mercedes, Volkswagen), machinery, and chemicals. The US exports aircraft, pharmaceuticals, mineral fuels, and tech products to Germany. The trade imbalance has been a persistent source of US political pressure on Germany.
disputes: US trade deficit concerns and tariff threats

diplomatic.status: Core allies with recurring transactional tensions
diplomatic.summary: Germany and the US are foundational NATO allies and core transatlantic partners. Under Trump 2.0 (2025-), the relationship has experienced significant strain over defense spending demands, Ukraine policy (US pressure for European ownership), tariff threats, and divergences on trade and tech regulation. Germany has accelerated European strategic autonomy efforts in response. The relationship remains vital but more transactional.
recent_incidents: US tariff threats on European automotive exports (2025)
US pressure on Germany's China trade relations

military.summary: Germany and the US are NATO's two largest European members by defense output. The US maintains ~35,000 troops in Germany (Ramstein AB, Grafenwohr). Germany has committed to 2%+ GDP defense spending following the Zeitenwende. Cooperation on Ukraine military assistance. Some friction over Trump-era pressure on NATO burden-sharing and F-35 program vs European fighter alternatives.

financial.summary: The US and Germany have massive bilateral investment relationships. US firms (Apple, Google, Amazon, pharmaceutical companies) have major German operations. German firms (SAP, Bayer, BASF, Volkswagen, BMW) have large US presences. Dollar-euro relationship makes US monetary policy highly consequential for Germany.

energy.summary: The US became a major LNG supplier to Germany after 2022 Russian energy decoupling. US LNG terminals supply German regasification plants. US-Germany cooperation on energy transition technology. Germany is a major US LNG customer, though this gives the US new trade leverage.

scientific.summary: The US-Germany scientific partnership is among the world's most productive. Joint research across virtually all fields — from CERN participation to AI, biomedical, aerospace, and climate science. Massive academic exchange program. German and American universities and research institutes maintain deep institutional ties.
